title: Error: lstat  operation not permitted when using --storage-opt size= with userns=auto with uid and gid bindings
body:
### Issue Description

Hi everyone

Since I am moving away from docker to podman, to start with I am trying to have my application to work as expected without changing much.

I used the following config for user namespaces to mimic `dockremap`. The most important feature I need here is to have all the container map 20000 to 0 inside the container. 

```
# /etc/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
userns = "auto:uidmapping=0:20000:65536,gidmapping=0:20000:65536"
```

When I run podman using `--storage-opt size=value` it fails with the following error:

```
Error: lstat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/28d80edcb7bcf69256a47d23c6a0dab25edb706e187318438941fb9f77895a30/merged/etc/passwd: operation not permitted
```

Changing the userns mode to `userns=auto` or to `userns=keep-id` worked fine.

Not sure if this is an unexpected behavior, or is it impossible by design? 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Configure userns mode to auto with uid/gid mappings.
```
# /etc/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
userns = "auto:uidmapping=0:20000:65536,gidmapping=0:20000:65536"
```

3. Run podman with --storage-opt size=value
```
podman run -it -d --entrypoint bash --storage-opt size=700M myimage:latest
```


### Describe the results you received

I got the following error:

```
Error: lstat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/28d80edcb7bcf69256a47d23c6a0dab25edb706e187318438941fb9f77895a30/merged/etc/passwd: operation not permitted
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the container to be run without errors and with user namespace mapping 20000:65536

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
using `--storage-opt` resets the settings from the `storage.conf` file and uses the default configuration.  So any setting you've configured there is ignored.

To mimic Docker remap though, you need to use a static configuration for the mappings, Docker does not support "auto" AFAIK.

Also, please try with the latest released version of Podman.  The issue tracker here is for upstream issues, not for old versions.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
